fixtures.name: paradigm_break_flask_routing
fixtures.rationale: FastAPI uses @app.get / @router.post method-specific decorators and Pydantic parameter injection; Flask's @app.route(..., methods=[...]), request.get_json(), request.args.get(), jsonify(), abort(), and app.run() are absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_django_cbv
fixtures.rationale: FastAPI uses function-based endpoints with typed parameter injection; Django class-based views (View subclasses with def get/post methods), request.body + json.loads(), JsonResponse, HttpResponseNotFound, and urlpatterns = path() have no equivalent in the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_aiohttp_handler
fixtures.rationale: FastAPI uses declarative method decorators with parameter injection; aiohttp's async def handler(request: web.Request), await request.json(), request.match_info, web.json_response(), web.HTTPNotFound(), and app.router.add_get() are entirely absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_manual_validation
fixtures.rationale: FastAPI's idiomatic pattern is Pydantic BaseModel parameter injection for automatic validation; accepting `body: dict = Body(...)` and then manually checking isinstance(), field membership, length, and type with if-raise blocks recreates what Pydantic provides automatically — this manual validation pattern is absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_raw_response
fixtures.rationale: FastAPI automatically serializes dicts and Pydantic models returned from endpoints; manually constructing Response(content=json.dumps(...)) and JSONResponse(content=jsonable_encoder(...)) at every call site bypasses the framework's serialization layer — this explicit construction pattern is absent from the FastAPI application-endpoint corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_subtle_wrong_exception
fixtures.rationale: decorators, Pydantic models, and Depends() are all idiomatic; the break is confined to the raise statements: ValueError, KeyError, and RuntimeError are raised instead of HTTPException — plain Python exceptions propagate as unhandled 500s rather than the intended 404/503 status codes, and this exception vocabulary is absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_subtle_manual_status_check
fixtures.rationale: FastAPI + httpx corpus uses response.raise_for_status() to propagate downstream errors; manually checking `if response.status_code >= 400: raise HTTPException(...)` at every call site instead of calling raise_for_status() is a structural pattern absent from the corpus — all tokens are individually present but the branching structure is non-idiomatic

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_subtle_sync_endpoint
fixtures.rationale: the FastAPI corpus is dominated by async def endpoints; sync def endpoints with blocking I/O (time.sleep, requests.get, blocking DB calls) block the event loop's thread pool — the decorator and Depends() vocabulary is correct but the sync + blocking-I/O combination is structurally absent from the corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_subtle_exception_swallow
fixtures.rationale: decorators, Pydantic models, Depends(), HTTPException, and async def are all idiomatic; the break is purely structural: every endpoint body is wrapped in try/except Exception: pass or except Exception as e: logger.warning — broad exception-swallowing is absent from the FastAPI corpus, which propagates errors or handles specific exception types

fixtures.name: control_router_endpoint
fixtures.rationale: idiomatic FastAPI: APIRouter with method-specific decorators, Pydantic BaseModel body params, typed path/query params with Query(), response_model= annotation, HTTPException(status_code=..., detail=...) on not-found, Depends() for DB session and auth — canonical pattern for CRUD endpoints

fixtures.name: control_dependency_injection
fixtures.rationale: idiomatic FastAPI dependency injection: generator dep with yield for session lifecycle, nested Depends() chain, Annotated[T, Depends(...)] shorthand, OAuth2PasswordBearer token extraction, sub-dependencies accepting their own Depends() params — all canonical DI patterns from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: control_exception_handling
fixtures.rationale: idiomatic FastAPI error handling: @app.exception_handler(RequestValidationError) returning JSONResponse with exc.errors(), @app.exception_handler(HTTPException) forwarding status_code and headers, raise HTTPException(404/401/403, detail=...) at endpoint call sites, HTTPException with headers={'WWW-Authenticate': 'Bearer'} — canonical pattern from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_starlette_mount
fixtures.rationale: bare Starlette Router with add_route() and url_for() instead of FastAPI's @router.get/@router.post DSL — imperative route registration with {id:int} path converters, no Pydantic and no Depends, is entirely absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_tornado_handler
fixtures.rationale: tornado RequestHandler subclass with GET/POST methods using self.get_argument(), self.write(), self.finish(), and IOLoop — classic tornado vocabulary entirely foreign to the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_marshmallow_schema
fixtures.rationale: marshmallow Schema.load() called manually inside endpoints for validation instead of Pydantic BaseModel injection — @validates, @post_load, and fields.String/Float are marshmallow idioms absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_cerberus_validation
fixtures.rationale: cerberus Validator with schema dicts validates plain dict body params instead of Pydantic BaseModel injection — v.errors, validator.validate(), and validator.normalized() are cerberus idioms absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_orjson_serialization
fixtures.rationale: explicit orjson.dumps() with OPT_INDENT_2/OPT_NON_STR_KEYS/OPT_SORT_KEYS options at every endpoint, wrapped in Response(..., media_type='application/json') — bypasses FastAPI's automatic serialization layer, pattern absent from the corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_bare_except
fixtures.rationale: every endpoint body wrapped in bare except: (no exception type) that silently returns empty data — broad exception-swallowing with no HTTPException or logging is an anti-pattern absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_event_loop_blocking
fixtures.rationale: asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete() called inside async def endpoint bodies — incorrect usage that raises RuntimeError inside a running loop, represents the wrong mental model of async programming absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_sync_requests_in_async
fixtures.rationale: synchronous requests.get() / requests.post() with Session() inside async def endpoints blocks the event loop — the correct pattern is httpx.AsyncClient with await, and requests.Session with cert=/verify= is absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_global_singletons
fixtures.rationale: module-level singletons (_db, _cache, _conn) accessed via get_db()/get_cache() called directly in endpoints without Depends() — global keyword to reassign singletons with no teardown or context management is absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_threading_background
fixtures.rationale: threading.Thread with daemon=True launched from each endpoint instead of FastAPI BackgroundTasks — Thread, Lock, daemon, and t.join() blocking the async event loop are all absent from the FastAPI corpus

fixtures.name: control_pydantic_validator
fixtures.rationale: idiomatic FastAPI query-param validation via Pydantic BaseModel + Field constraints + Query() injection — grounded in corpus docs_src/query_param_models pattern, no @field_validator

fixtures.name: control_response_model
fixtures.rationale: response_model= annotation on every endpoint so FastAPI validates and serializes output — endpoints return plain dicts or Pydantic models with no manual json.dumps() or explicit Response construction

fixtures.name: control_async_streaming
fixtures.rationale: async request handling with custom APIRoute and await — grounded in corpus docs_src/custom_request_and_route pattern using Request, Response, HTTPException, RequestValidationError

fixtures.name: control_httpx_async
fixtures.rationale: httpx.AsyncClient as a context manager in a Depends() dependency with await client.get() and response.raise_for_status() — canonical async downstream HTTP pattern, no synchronous requests library

fixtures.name: control_annotated_depends
fixtures.rationale: Annotated[T, Depends(...)] TypeAlias shorthand for all dependency injection with yield-based resource lifecycle — modern FastAPI DI pattern, deeply in-distribution with 646 Annotated occurrences in the corpus
